title: Claude session handoff: find previous session JSONL and extract context
desc: Finds the previous Claude Code session JSONL file for the current project under ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/, reads the last ~120 lines, and extracts human/assistant text turns to reconstruct handoff context. Run at the start of a new session when the user asks to continue from the last session.
steps: 1. Derive project slug: convert CWD path (drive letter + slashes) to hyphens, e.g. D:/GitHub/WkAutoQuant -> d--GitHub-WkAutoQuant
2. List JSONL files sorted by mtime descending: ls -lt ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/*.jsonl
head -5. Current session is index [0], previous session is index [1]
3. Read last 120 lines of the previous session JSONL via PowerShell: Get-Content <path> -Encoding UTF8 -Tail 120
4. For each line parse JSON; extract role + message.content text fields. Skip blank lines, API errors, system hooks. Print [role] <text[:600]> per substantive turn
5. Synthesize handoff summary: last user request, last assistant state, unfinished action items, pending confirmations, open file edits
6. Report to user: what was happening, what was left unfinished, proposed next step

requirements.expect: lists 5 JSONL files with timestamps; [0]=current session [1]=previous session

requirements.cmd: Get-Content <prev.jsonl> -Encoding UTF8 -Tail 120 piped through ConvertFrom-Json per line
requirements.expect: extracts role+text turns without mojibake, skips API errors and empty lines
output contains last user request summary plus unfinished action items list plus proposed next step; never outputs raw JSONL

author: claude
